channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: After Global Economy Shudders, Trump Zigzags on Whether War Is Nearing End
item.description: The president told reporters at a news conference in Florida that the fighting is “going to be ended soon” but added that the U.S. would strike Iran harder if needed.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times

item.title: Fear and Hope for Iranians Trapped Between Bombs and Defiant Rulers
item.description: Many in Iran feel helpless in the face of their entrenched system, and some are becoming increasingly embittered by the fierce American and Israeli bombardment.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon, Kiana Hayeri and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Apocalyptic scenes of bombardment, like at an oil-storage facility in the Tehran area on Sunday, have contributed to many Iranians’ gloominess about their nation’s future.

item.title: On the Road With Zelensky, Weathered, Weary and Fighting On
item.description: Ukraine’s leader, Volodymyr Zelensky, traveled east to visit frontline troops trying to stave off Russian attacks, and invited reporters for The New York Times to go with him.
item.dc:creator: Kim Barker and Brendan Hoffman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine in Kramatorsk on Friday. “They need a lot of things,” he said of the soldiers. “And each day needs are changing.”

item.title: Trial of Ekrem Imamoglu, Erdogan’s Rival, Begins in Turkey
item.description: Ekrem Imamoglu, the former mayor of Istanbul who stands accused of corruption, clashed with the judge and criticized the trial as baseless.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard and Safak Timur
item.media:credit: Burak Kara/Getty Images
item.media:description: Supporters of Ekrem Imamoglu, the former mayor of Istanbul, protesting outside the Silivri prison and courthouse on Monday.

item.title: Iran Choosing Mojtaba Khamenei as Supreme Leader Signals Defiance Amid War
item.description: Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei could prove to be even more radical than his father and predecessor, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed by the U.S. and Israel at the start of the war.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: People gathered in Tehran on Monday, carrying flags and posters of Mojtaba Khamenei, to show support for Iran’s supreme leader.

item.title: Ukraine Helps U.S. Bases in the Mideast With Stopping Drones
item.description: As the war in Iran spreads, Kyiv is eagerly offering its hard-won expertise and advanced technology to counter Iranian drones.
item.dc:creator: Kim Barker
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tyler Hicks/The New York Times
item.media:description: Soldiers in the Donetsk region of Ukraine in December, monitoring the sky to shoot down Russian Shahed drones using a 30-millimeter antiaircraft gun.

item.title: Is the Trial of Erdogan’s Top Rival More About Corruption or Politics?
item.description: Prosecutors accuse the former Istanbul mayor Ekrem Imamoglu of leading a criminal scheme. His supporters say Turkey’s president is trying to eliminate a political foe.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard, Gulsin Harman and Safak Timur
item.media:credit: Burak Kara/Getty Images
item.media:description: Demonstrators in Istanbul showing support for Ekrem Imamoglu, then the mayor of Istanbul, last March after he was arrested.

item.title: The Suburb That Won’t Sleep
item.description: Food is bringing thousands of people together to an unexpected place: a plaza west of Toronto. Some fear it’s tearing a neighborhood apart.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai
item.media:credit: Ian Willms for The New York Times
item.media:description: The city of Mississauga, Ontario, has imposed steep fines on the companies that own the plaza for failing to stop gatherings with fireworks and loud music. Nearby residents also complain about heavy traffic.

item.title: Explosion Damages Synagogue in Belgium
item.description: Officials described the blast, which caused no injuries, as an antisemitic act, but the exact circumstances were still under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Koba Ryckewaert
item.media:credit: John Thys/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Police officers guarding the area near a synagogue that was damaged by an explosion in Liège, Belgium, on Monday.

item.title: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards: The Spine of a Militarized State
item.description: With their pervasive military, political and economic clout, the Guards are often considered the main impediment to regime change, or any change, in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps members in Tehran in 2024, commemorating Palestinian and Lebanese militants killed by Israel.

item.title: Gen Z-Backed Rapper Is on Course to Lead Nepal With Landslide Win
item.description: A youth-led uprising brought Nepal’s government down last year. Now, a 35-year-old politician demanding change is set to become prime minister.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech, Sajal Pradhan, Binod Ghimire and Hannah Reyes Morales
item.media:credit: Hannah Reyes Morales for The New York Times
item.media:description: Balendra Shah, center, during his campaign to become the next prime minister of Nepal last Saturday. To his right is Rabi Lamichhane, the Rastriya Swatantra Party president.

item.title: A Loose Band of Emerging Powers Is Divided Over Iran
item.description: Brazil, China and Russia all denounced the U.S.-Israeli attacks, but other nations in the BRICS group haven’t, even though Iran is a fellow member.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ionova
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eraldo Peres/Associated Press
item.media:description: Premier Li Qiang of China, Prime Minister Narendra Modi of India, President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva of Brazil and President Cyril Ramaphosa of South Africa at a BRICS summit in Rio de Janeiro last year.

item.title: Ahead of Colombia’s Elections, Violence on the Campaign Trail Rattles Politicians
item.description: Politicians in Colombia have increasingly become targets of violence. A rise in kidnappings, death threats and assassinations has shaken the country ahead of the vote.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní and Federico Rios
item.media:credit: Federico Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: Police officers deployed across Popayan, Colombia, are monitoring security ahead of legislative elections on Sunday.

item.title: In Russia’s Former Capital, Restoring the Past to Survive the Present
item.description: With political activism off-limits, residents of St. Petersburg are finding purpose and community in the “politics of small deeds,” repairing and cleaning architectural treasures.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Nechepurenko and Mary Gelman
item.media:credit: Mary Gelman for The New York Times

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron, Farnaz Fassihi and Yan Zhuang

item.title: Cancer Haunts Neighbors of Canada’s Oil Sands Wastelands
item.description: Though high rates of the disease persist among the nearby Indigenous communities, the Canadian government is weighing rules that may allow energy giants to release treated mining waste into the river system.
item.dc:creator: Emily Baumgaertner Nunn
item.media:credit: Pat Kane for The New York Times
item.media:description: A Syncrude facility near Fort McMurray, Alberta.

item.title: This is what happened on March 9.
As War Comes to Gulf States, Migrant Workers Pay the Highest Price
item.description: Since the American-Israeli attack on Iran began, at least 11 civilians have been killed in oil-rich Gulf countries. All but one of them were foreign nationals.
item.dc:creator: Zia ur-Rehman, Vivian Nereim and Aie Balagtas See
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Christopher Pike/Getty Images
item.media:description: Smoke rising last week after an explosion in an industrial zone of Fujairah, United Arab Emirates.

item.title: U.S. Solar Installations Fell in 2025 as Trump Attacked Clean Energy
item.description: More solar energy was added to U.S. grids than any other technology, but the amount installed fell by 14 percent, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Penn
item.media:credit: Mason Trinca for The New York Times
item.media:description: A solar field in Riesel, Texas, in 2023. Solar power is projected to account for just over half of the new power projects that will be added this year.

item.title: Missile in Deadly Iranian School Strike Appears to Be U.S.-Made, Photos Taken by Iran Show
item.description: Iranian state media posted mangled remnants it claims were from the Feb. 28 attack in Minab. An analysis shows they have the markings of a missile made by American manufacturers
item.dc:creator: Christiaan Triebert, Malachy Browne and John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Who Is Balendra Shah, The Rapper Set to Be Nepal’s Next Prime Minister?
item.description: Balendra Shah’s party won a landslide in the election that followed Nepal’s Gen Z revolution. His style is pugnacious.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech, Binod Ghimire and Sajal Pradhan
item.media:credit: Hannah Reyes Morales for The New York Times
item.media:description: Balendra Shah campaigning in Kathmandu, Nepal, last month.

item.title: Trump Seeks to Calm Oil and Gas Markets but Says Iran War Will Go On
item.description: The president said the U.S. could accompany tankers through the Strait of Hormuz if necessary to keep oil flowing. “We’ve already won in many ways, but we haven’t won enough,” he said.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Benoit Tessier/Reuters
item.media:description: Oil tankers sat anchored in Muscat, Oman, on Saturday as Iran vowed to block the Strait of Hormuz amid the conflict.

item.title: Shells Fired From Lebanon Land West of Syrian Capital, Country’s Military Says
item.description: The Syrian military accused Hezbollah of targeting Syrian army positions outside of Damascus.
item.dc:creator: Dayana Iwaza and Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Khalil Ashawi/Reuters
item.media:description: President Ahmed al-Shara at a conference last month in Damascus. On Monday, he called the escalation in the Middle East “an existential threat to the entire region.”

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the Conflict on Monday
item.description: President Trump gave conflicting signals about a possible end to the war with Iran amid wild gyrations in global markets.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump speaking from Doral, Fla., on Monday, initially said the war with Iran was “very complete, pretty much.”

item.title: Oil Prices and Stocks Swing Wildly as Iran and Israel Launch New Strikes
item.description: Oil prices and stocks initially swung wildly, and Iran’s foreign minister promised “surprises,” calling the strikes against his country “Operation Epic Mistake.”
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Farnaz Fassihi, Richard Pérez-Peña and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: People in Tehran showing their support on Monday for Mojtaba Khamenei, who was selected as Iran’s new supreme leader.

item.title: Australia Grants Humanitarian Visas to 5 Members of Iranian Women’s Soccer Team
item.description: Concern for the safety of the players had grown after Iranian state media criticized them for not singing the national anthem at a game in Australia.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim
item.media:credit: Dave Hunt/AAPIMAGE, via Associated Press
item.media:description: The Iranian national women’s soccer team played the Philippines in its last match in the Asian Cup tournament in Australia on Sunday.

item.title: Ukraine Lends a Hand
item.description: Countries targeted by Iranian drones are eager to tap into Ukrainian expertise to shoot them down.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman for The New York Times

item.title: Oman Becomes Unlikely Hub for Evacuees Fleeing War in Gulf
item.description: The international airport in Muscat, the Omani capital, has remained open while fighting in the region has escalated.
item.dc:creator: Turki Al-Balushi and Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Vadim Ghirda/Associated Press
item.media:description: Passengers from an evacuation flight from Muscat, Oman, at Henri Coanda International Airport, just north of Bucharest, Romania, last week.

item.title: France Is Sending a Large Naval Force to the Middle East
item.description: President Emmanuel Macron said the warships would help protect France’s allies in the region, and could be part of a force to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz. He said the war could continue for “several days, maybe several weeks.”
item.dc:creator: Catherine Porter and Ana Castelain
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Gonzalo Fuentes
item.media:description: President Emmanuel Macron, center right, visiting the French aircraft carrier Charles de Gaulle during a visit to Cyprus on Monday.

item.title: U.S. Reaches Tentative Deal Ending Prosecution of Turkish Bank
item.description: Court papers submitted as part of the agreement with Halkbank, which was accused of doing business with Iranian entities, said Turkey’s assistance “was instrumental” in the Israel-Hamas cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett
item.media:credit: Umit Bektas/Reuters
item.media:description: President Recep Tayyip Erdogan of Turkey has long pressed President Trump to quash the case against the Turkish bank.

item.title: At a cemetery in Tehran, there are fresh graves and angry slogans.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Women crying at the burial of a relative who was killed by U.S.-Israeli airstrikes, in Tehran, Iran’s capital.

item.title: Police Rescue 23 People Stranded on Moving Ice in Canada
item.description: Nearly two dozen people in Canada were stranded on ice that separated from shore. “We honestly thought we were doomed,” one fisherman said.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather
item.media:credit: Ontario Provincial Police
item.media:description: Nearly two dozen people were rescued after they were stranded on breakaway ice by Georgian Bay, north of Toronto, on Sunday.

item.title: Video Captures Apparent Strike Near Boys’ School in Iran
item.description: A communications tower seemed to be the intended target, according to satellite imagery analysis. Iranian state media reported that a boy had been killed in the explosion.
item.dc:creator: Christiaan Triebert and Parin Behrooz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: How The New York Times Reports on War in Iran
item.description: Staying safe and corroborating facts are among the challenges for journalists working under intense pressure around the clock and the world.
item.dc:creator: Mike Abrams
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A rally to mourn the supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in Tehran.

item.title: Countries in Asia Try to Contain the Economic Fallout of the Mideast Conflict
item.description: Across Asia, where countries are highly exposed to rising oil and gas costs and tightening supply, governments are acting to mitigate economic harm.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Krolik, Choe Sang-Hun, Sui-Lee Wee, Meaghan Tobin and Damien Cave
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Benoit Tessier/Reuters
item.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz is a narrow passage between Iran and Oman. Historically 20 million barrels pass through it each day, most of it bound for Asia.

item.title: NATO Intercepts Second Iranian Missile Entering Turkey’s Airspace, Defense Ministry Says
item.description: It was the second time in six days that Turkey announced the interception of a missile from Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Michaela Stache/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Mark Rutte, NATO’s secretary general, said the alliance did not need to activate its mutual defense clause.

item.title: Dozens Wounded in Bahrain as Arab States Condemn Iranian Strikes
item.description: Key U.S. allies in the Persian Gulf have stepped up their criticism of Iran after facing a barrage of missiles and drone attacks over the past week.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Reuters
item.media:description: Smoke rising from a refinery operated by Bapco Energies, Bahrain’s state-owned energy company, on Sitra Island, Bahrain, on Monday.

item.title: Ed Davey Wants King Charles to Cancel his U.S. State Visit Over Iran War
item.description: Ed Davey, the leader of the Liberal Democrats in Britain, said President Trump does not deserve the “diplomatic coup” of hosting the king for a state visit.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:credit: Andrew Matthews/Press Association, via Associated Press
item.media:description: King Charles III was expected to travel to the United States to meet President Trump next month, as part of celebrations for the 250th anniversary of independence.

item.title: Israeli Forces Raid New Area in Southern Lebanon, Bombard Near Beirut
item.description: Joseph Aoun, the Lebanese president, called for direct talks with Israel to end the fighting. He also leveled blame at Hezbollah, the Iran-backed group, for igniting the war.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: An airstrike in the Dahiya neighborhood in the southern outskirts of Beirut, in Lebanon, on Monday.

item.title: Australia Urged to Protect Members of the Iranian Women’s Soccer Team
item.description: The athletes, who are set to head home, were called “traitors” by Iran’s state media after they didn’t sing their national anthem during a match in Australia.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Dave Hunt/Australian Associated Press, via Reuters
item.media:description: Members of Iran’s women’s soccer team before a match on Sunday night in Australia.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron, Farnaz Fassihi, Aaron Boxerman, Anton Troianovski and Richard Pérez-Peña

item.title: Stocks Rebound After Trump Says Iran War Is ‘Very Complete’
item.description: The S&P 500 ended slightly higher on Monday as investors interpreted President Trump’s comments as a signal that the conflict could end soon.
item.dc:creator: Joe Rennison, River Akira Davis, Meaghan Tobin and Eshe Nelson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kazuhiro Nogi/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: For China, Billions of Dollars Are at Risk From a Widening War
item.description: The country found a home in the Middle East for its investments and growing markets for steel, electric vehicles and solar panels. Those are now at stake.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson and Murphy Zhao
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: The New York Times
item.media:description: Beilun Port in China. The Middle East became even more important to China after China’s trade rivalry with the United States escalated.

item.title: Glasgow Train Station Is Shut Down After Fire Tears Through Neighboring Shop
item.description: The fire started in a commercial building on Sunday afternoon, and firefighters worked through the night to contain it. Train service was disrupted on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang and Claire Moses
item.media:credit: Jeff J Mitchell/Getty Images
item.media:description: More than 60 firefighters battled the blaze after it began at a shop near Glasgow’s main train station on Sunday.

item.title: Democrats Seize on Oil Topping $100 a Barrel, as Trump Downplays Jump
item.description: The global oil benchmark topped $100 a barrel late Sunday, putting Republicans on the defensive on an issue at the center of this year’s midterm elections.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Ken Cedeno/Reuters
item.media:description: President Trump immediately sought to downplay the spike in oil prices.

item.title: U.S. Tomahawk Hit Naval Base Beside Iranian School, Video Shows
item.description: The evidence contradicts President Trump’s claim that Iran was responsible for a strike at the school that killed 175 people, most of them children.
item.dc:creator: Malachy Browne and John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Iran’s Security Establishment Celebrates Mojtaba Khamenei’s Selection
item.description: Opponents of the government, however, worried the new supreme leader, a son of the recently killed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, would rule with an iron fist like his father.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A poster of Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei in Tehran on the night he was announced as the new supreme leader of Iran.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the Conflict on Sunday
item.description: Iran named Mojtaba Khamenei, a son of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, as his father’s successor. The Pentagon announced the death of a seventh U.S. service member.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Smoke plumes rising from an oil storage site in Tehran, the Iranian capital, on Sunday.

item.title: This is what happened on March 8.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Who Is Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran’s New Supreme Leader?
item.description: The selection of a son of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in a strike on his compound at the start of the war, could anger Iranians seeking change.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni, Sanam Mahoozi and Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Office of The Iranian Supreme Leader/Wana News Agency, via Reuters
item.media:description: A handout image from Iran state media of Mojtaba Khamenei, a son of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in Tehran in 2024.

item.title: Iran Has a New Supreme Leader. What Does That Mean?
item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei takes on a role that makes him not only Iran’s spiritual leader but also the highest authority in the land.
item.dc:creator: Carlotta Gall
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Rouzbeh Fouladi/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Mojtaba Khamenei, center, at a rally in Tehran in 2019.

item.title: Sizing Up Iran’s Elite Guards
item.description: Many see the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps as the main barrier to regime change, or any change at all, in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Mojtaba Khamenei, Son of Iran’s Slain Supreme Leader, Is a Mysterious Figure
item.description: The succession of the slain leader’s son is seen as a signal of the Islamic republic’s defiance of Israel and the United States, and of continuity during crisis.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Rouzbeh Fouladi/Middle East Images, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Mojtaba Khamenei, center, the son of the Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in 2019.

item.title: State Dept. Said to Order Diplomats in Saudi Arabia to Leave
item.description: The ordered departure of U.S. employees in the kingdom indicates that senior diplomats are bracing for a possible surge in violence in the war with Iran, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong, Mark Mazzetti, Vivian Nereim and Adam Rasgon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: -/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: The U.S. Embassy in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia.

item.title: Pentagon Announces Seventh U.S. Death in War With Iran
item.description: The service member killed was not publicly identified, but U.S. Central Command said the death was caused by injuries after an attack on a Saudi military base.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt and Helene Cooper
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: The first six American service members killed in the war with Iran were returned to Dover Air Force Base in Delaware on Saturday.

item.title: U.S. Continues Airstrikes Against a Wide Array of Targets in Iran
item.description: A senior U.S. military official said those targets included missile launchers and air-defense sites.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay, Eric Schmitt and Johnatan Reiss
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Smoke rising in Tehran on Sunday after airstrikes.

item.title: Lebanese Villagers Bury Hezbollah Fighters, Praising Their Defiance of Israel
item.description: After a fierce ground fight between Israeli forces and Iran-backed Hezbollah, the village of Nabi Sheet mourned its dead. “We are not here to bow,” said one villager. “We will not kneel.”
item.dc:creator: Sarah Chaayto, David Guttenfelder and Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: David Guttenfelder/The New York Times
item.media:description: Mourners at a funeral in Nabi Sheet, Lebanon.
